skill.name: zach-sif-cvr-threshold-analyzer
skill.description: Analyze Amazon ASIN daily business reports and user-provided SIF keyword rank exports. The skill backtests CVR against organic rank volatility and produces observation, danger, and ad CVR confirmation thresholds.
skill.summary: Backtest CVR thresholds against Amazon organic keyword rank volatility for one ASIN.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical heuristic combinations, but review found no prompt injection, network exfiltration, credential harvesting, or malicious code execution. Most backtick, entropy, path traversal, weak crypto, and network alerts are false positives from Markdown examples, Chinese documentation, XML namespaces, and normal parsing code. The remaining risk is medium because the skill enables Bash-assisted script execution and reads or writes local user-specified files.

medium_findings.title: Local File Read and Write Capability
medium_findings.description: The analysis script accepts user-controlled report, SIF cache, and output paths. This is expected for local analytics, but users could expose sensitive local files or write reports outside an intended workspace if paths are chosen poorly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly opens paths supplied through CLI arguments and writes multiple output files. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but the filesystem capability is real.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Script Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash and documents Python command execution to run the bundled analyzer. No shell interpolation or dynamic command construction was found, but external command capability increases operational risk in an AI skill context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata includes Bash and the README contains explicit Python execution examples. I found no evidence of command injection or hidden shell execution.

low_findings.title: XLSX Archive Parsing Resource Risk
low_findings.description: The script uses zipfile to parse workbook internals when openpyxl is unavailable. This is normal XLSX handling, but very large or malformed workbooks could cause local resource use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The archive parsing is visible and legitimate for XLSX support. The risk is limited to local resource exhaustion from untrusted workbook inputs.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown and Entropy Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: Backtick execution, weak crypto, path traversal, high entropy, and dangerous-combination alerts were mostly caused by Markdown examples, inline code, Chinese prose, filenames, and report text. No evidence found for Ruby backtick execution, credential access, obfuscation, or evasion logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations are documentation tables, fenced examples, and inline code literals. They do not execute code or hide binary content.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: The single network alert is an XML namespace string used when constructing a test workbook. No HTTP client, socket usage, or outbound request path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a static XML namespace in test data, not in network code. A targeted search found no request libraries or socket calls.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Local Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reads input paths and writes output paths selected by the user. Users should run it only on intended reports and workspace directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path arguments and output writes are explicit in the script. They are part of the intended local workflow, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Offsite traffic can lower CVR and make organic rank changes harder to interpret. This skill compares business reports with SIF rank exports and creates practical warning thresholds.
actual_capabilities: Reads local CSV, XLSX, or XLSM daily ASIN business reports.
Reads local SIF daily keyword rank JSON exported by user tooling.
Filters one target ASIN and validates continuous daily report dates.
Computes observation, danger, and ad CVR confirmation threshold candidates.
Generates Markdown, CSV, and JSON outputs for review.
Flags small samples, missing SIF days, and negative organic adjustment days.
limitations: It does not call SIF services or fetch rank data by itself.
It does not prove causation between CVR and organic rank movement.
It needs enough continuous daily data to produce stable thresholds.
It does not modify ads, budgets, prices, coupons, or listings.

use_cases.title: Review offsite campaign risk
use_cases.description: Compare CVR drops with organic rank volatility after influencer, affiliate, or content traffic starts.

use_cases.title: Create daily monitoring lines
use_cases.description: Build observation and danger thresholds for operators who watch rank movement during traffic expansion.

use_cases.title: Audit traffic quality signals
use_cases.description: Use ad CVR confirmation to check whether poor conversion is broader than offsite traffic dilution.

prompt_templates.title: Run the sample analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation with bundled example files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use zach-sif-cvr-threshold-analyzer with the bundled sample business report and SIF cache. Show me the generated threshold summary and output file paths.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze one ASIN
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard workflow with user-provided local files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze ASIN B0XXXXXXX for the US site using my local business report and SIF daily keyword cache. Use my launch date and explain the observation, danger, and ad CVR confirmation lines.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate weak thresholds
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review when the report has concerns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the generated threshold report and identify why any line was not robust. Check sample size, missing SIF days, event counts, precision, recall, and Lift.

prompt_templates.title: Compare operating actions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced decision support after multiple threshold candidates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the candidate CSV and daily panel to compare conservative and aggressive CVR warning thresholds. Recommend which metrics need human review before budget or listing changes.

output_examples.input: A 30-day business report, a matching SIF rank cache, one ASIN, and a launch date.
output_examples.output: A report naming the best observation line, danger line, ad CVR confirmation line, sample concerns, and generated attachments.

output_examples.input: A short 12-day sample window with complete reports and rank data.
output_examples.output: A completed analysis that warns the sample is too small for strong operating decisions.

output_examples.input: A report with low CVR days and several core keyword drops.
output_examples.output: A daily panel showing threshold triggers, rank volatility events, and review points for traffic quality.

best_practices: Use one ASIN per run and confirm the ASIN filter before reviewing thresholds.
Provide at least 21 continuous daily rows when using results for decisions.
Review budget, price, promotion, and listing actions manually before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the thresholds as Amazon official ranking rules.
Do not mix multiple ASINs in one report window.
Do not publish raw store reports or SIF exports in shared output folders.

faq.question: Does this skill fetch SIF data automatically?
faq.answer: No. It reads a local SIF JSON cache exported by the user or their own tooling.

faq.question: Can it read Excel reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports CSV, XLSX, and XLSM business reports with compatible daily fields.

faq.question: What are the three threshold lines?
faq.answer: They are an observation line, a danger line, and an ad CVR confirmation line.

faq.question: Does it change advertising settings?
faq.answer: No. It only creates reports and suggested review points for humans.

faq.question: Why can a threshold be missing?
faq.answer: A line can be missing when sample size, events, precision, recall, or Lift are not strong enough.

faq.question: Is a low CVR threshold proof of ranking causation?
faq.answer: No. It is a correlation-based warning signal that requires business review.
